annotations.decision: history of peripheral vascular disease and end-stage renal disease
She called her primary care physician, [**Name10 (NameIs) 1023**] sent her to the Emergency Room
Coronary artery disease status post myocardial infarction
status post aorto to right femoral bypass graft.
Preoperative chest x-ray for checking her Swan placement showed the femoral access Swan-Ganz catheter in normal position, intra-aortic balloon pump was in position
There were no pleural effusions or pneumothoraces and no evidence of acute cardiopulmonary disease.
Preoperative EKG showed sinus rhythm at 81 with a right bundle branch block and no significant change from her prior EKGs at admission.
In the Cath Laboratory, results showed significant coronary disease with a 90% mid LAD lesion and 90% proximal circumflex lesion
Nipride was started for afterload reduction
Cardiology inserted an intra-aortic balloon pump
patient was seen by Dr. [**First Name4 (NamePattern1) 18078**] [**Last Name (NamePattern1) 14968**] with Cardiothoracic Surgery
99% lesion of the LAD at the takeoff of a major diagonal.
The RCA had a patent stent with 40% distal lesion.
Patient was referred to Dr. [**First Name8 (NamePattern2) **] [**Last Name (NamePattern1) 70**] of Cardiothoracic Surgery service.
Patient was followed by her attending cardiologist, Dr. [**First Name8 (NamePattern2) **] [**Last Name (NamePattern1) **]
stress dosed steroids and remained on Lasix as well as IV nitroglycerin with a wedge goal of 14-18
Nipride was stopped and kept in reserve if needed
admitted to the CCU from the Catheterization Laboratory.
Integrilin drip as well as Heparin drip
Her metoprolol was increased to 50 b.i.d.
started on captopril 12.5 t.i.d. and also remained on nitroglycerin drip
receiving her renal dosing of cyclosporin
single episode of flash pulmonary edema
successfully treated in the Catheterization Laboratory.
preliminary noninvasive carotid studies showed an occluded left ICA and a right ICA narrowing of less than 40%.
Patient was seen by Vascular Surgery
Dr. [**Last Name (STitle) 48367**] of Vascular Surgery determined there is no role for vascular surgery at this particular time
patient underwent coronary artery bypass grafting x2 by Dr. [**Last Name (STitle) 70**] with a LIMA to the LAD and a vein graft to the OM.
The patient still had her preoperative intra-aortic balloon pump in place
Neo-Synephrine drip on 1 mcg/kg/minute, insulin at 6 units/hour, and a propofol titrated to 10 mcg/kg/minute.
remained on CPAP with pressure support in the low 90s
low 90s in sinus rhythm with a pressure of 144/70.
Her heart was regular rate and rhythm
She had scattered rhonchi throughout her lungs
Her extremities were warm with 1+ edema.
Sedation was minimized with a plan to wean extubate her and D/C her chest tubes
Captopril was started 6.25 t.i.d.  Lasix diuresis was begun
received 1 amp of bicarb to balance her
Her intra-aortic balloon pump remained in place
She was seen by the Cardiology fellow for pulling her balloon pump on the 20th, which was done without any complications.
she was extubated.  Her balloon pump was out.
Her Neo-Synephrine was off as well as her nitroglycerin IV drip
She was in sinus rhythm in the 80s
Her white count rose slightly to 18.  Hematocrit remained stable at 26.4, but her platelets dropped again to 76,000.
Patient's prednisone 5 mg p.o. q.d. was restarted.
she remained in sinus rhythm in the 60s as her beta blockade began
She continued to receive Lasix, and her CellCept was restarted as well as the cyclosporin A.
She was seen by the Renal fellow
seen by the clinical nutrition team for screening followed by the social worker as well as the case manager.
heart was regular rate and rhythm
Her thyroid medicine had also been restarted the day prior
Recommendations were made and evaluated daily by the CT Surgery team
Patient was treated and decision was made that the patient would need an insulin drip overnight
team decided to transfer the patient back to CSRU
she continued to be followed in CSRU
Her creatinine dropped again slightly to 1.1
Her blood sugar continued to be up and down
She received Levaquin for a UTI
decision was made to transfer her back to [**Hospital Ward Name 121**] 2
She was seen by the [**Last Name (un) **] attending as well as the Renal fellow again,
patient was maintaining a good blood pressure 143/69 in sinus rhythm at 85 and was hemodynamically stable
She was alert and oriented in no distress.
Her wounds were clean, dry, and intact
Her belly was soft, nontender, nondistended.
Her temporary pacing wires were discontinued
[**Last Name (un) **] continued to follow the patient for blood sugar control.
Patient continued to progress well with ambulation with slight desatting, but recovered significantly
She was asymptomatic when she walked with Physical Therapy and was almost back to her baseline level.
hemodynamically stable with a good blood pressure
Her lungs were clear bilaterally with the exception of some diminished sounds at her right base
Heart was regular rate and rhythm
Her incisions were clean, dry, and intact.
Her chest x-ray showed a right basilar pneumothorax with a left pleural effusion.
Was taking p.o. and was tolerating them well
The follow-up chest x-ray showed no pneumothorax.
She remained on all of her medications
Status post coronary artery bypass grafting x2
Severe three vessel coronary artery disease with acute myocardial infarction and cardiogenic shock
Diabetic nephropathy with end-stage renal disease
Status post right aorto to right femoral bypass

following up with her [**Last Name (un) **] physicians immediately for correct protocol dosing.
